what_it_does: Implements pure, data-driven predicates and classifiers that assign a capability flag to one of five postures (locked, hazard, consent, safe, unmatched) based on injected SignalSets data, plus a function that derives the transport-invariant flag set from a consumer's spawn template.
observations: All rule logic is parameterized over SignalSets data and contains no platform-name literals.
R1 checks membership in signal_sets.transport_invariants, which is expected to be derived externally.
R2 scans only the flag name for hazard signal words and asserts hazard_scope is 'flag_name_only'.
R3 returns both a boolean and the matching capability axis name for auditability.
classify runs rules at priority R1 > R2 > R3 > R5, defaulting to R5 safe.
classify_with_novelty adds the R4 unmatched fail-closed path for novel or rail-incompatible flags.
teaching_refusal_for_r4 builds a structured refusal message with why, alternative, and host/rail boundary.
derive_transport_invariants unions unconditional head flags with body_key_override flags, then applies the R6 swap-kind head-default exclusion using capability_axes membership.
_flags_in_cmd extracts dash-prefixed tokens from a command list without interpreting values.
standouts: The R6 swap-kind head-default exclusion is computed from capability_axes data rather than a hardcoded list, preserving the no-platform-literals invariant.
classify returns posture, firing rule, and axis, making every decision auditable.
R4 is explicitly fail-closed and surfaced rather than silently dropped or allowed.
The file is heavily annotated with spec references and invariants.
conventions: Uses type hints and detailed docstrings.
Keeps functions pure and dependent only on passed arguments.
Marks internal helper with a leading underscore.
Uses set operations for invariant derivation.
Avoids embedded string literals for platform-specific concepts.
concerns: r2_hazard raises ValueError if hazard_scope is not 'flag_name_only', which could break on future data changes.
derive_transport_invariants calls head_builder() synchronously and trusts it to produce the minimal unconditional head.
No validation that body_key_overrides entries actually contain string flag values.
classify_with_novelty depends on external is_novel and rail_incompatible inputs whose derivation is not shown.
notes: This is the platform-agnostic, data-only rule engine that decides flag postures and derives transport invariants without embedding any platform-specific names.
questions: What is the exact type/structure of the signal_sets object passed to the rules?
Where and how is the is_novel flag computed during registry refresh?
How does the Level-2 platform binding guarantee that head_builder emits only the unconditional head?
What is the full content of the referenced spec sections (3.3, 2.4a, 3.7)?
Where does PlatformRegistry live and how does it populate signal_sets.transport_invariants?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it implements the five classification rules and the transport-invariant derivation as pure, data-driven functions.
apparent_themes: Fail-closed handling of unknown or incompatible flags
Deriving truth from the consumer's spawn template rather than hand-curated lists
intention_signals: It appears designed to enforce posture decisions using only injected signal data and the consumer's own spawn template, preventing the rule engine from encoding platform-specific opinions.
novelty: The R6 head-default exclusion is derived from capability-axis membership rather than a hardcoded exclusion set, keeping the file free of platform-specific flag literals.
contribution: A reusable, data-only classifier and transport-invariant derivation routine that a fused system could retain to make flag posture decisions without embedding platform-specific flag names.
summary_for_embedding: A platform-agnostic rule engine that classifies capability flags into locked, hazard, consent, safe, or unmatched postures using injected signal sets and derives transport-invariant sets from a consumer spawn template.
intention_for_embedding: To separate platform-neutral classification logic from platform-specific signal data so that flag posture decisions remain reproducible and free of embedded platform literals.

symbols.does: Checks whether a flag name is in the transport-invariant set.
symbols.description: Returns True if flag_name is a member of signal_sets.transport_invariants, treating such flags as locked regardless of other signals.

symbols.does: Checks whether a flag name contains a hazard signal word.
symbols.description: Scans only the flag name for words in signal_sets.hazard_name_vocabulary and raises ValueError if hazard_scope is not 'flag_name_only'.

symbols.does: Checks whether a flag belongs to a declared capability axis.
symbols.description: Iterates signal_sets.capability_axes and returns a tuple of (matched, axis_name), enabling auditable consent classification.

symbols.does: Assigns a posture by running R1, R2, R3, then defaulting to R5.
symbols.description: Returns a tuple of (posture, posture_rule, axis) using priority R1 > R2 > R3 > R5; R5 is the expose default for known flags.

symbols.does: Extends classify with the R4 fail-closed path for novel or rail-incompatible flags.
symbols.description: Defers to classify, then overrides R5 to R4 unmatched when the flag is novel or rail-incompatible, ensuring unknown flags are surfaced, not silently exposed.

symbols.does: Builds a structured refusal message for an R4 unmatched flag.
symbols.description: Composes a fail-closed, loud message explaining why the flag is refused, any alternative path, and the host/rail boundary.

symbols.does: Extracts dash-prefixed tokens from a command list.
symbols.description: Internal helper that returns elements starting with '-' so the transport-invariant derivation can identify head flags without parsing values.

symbols.does: Derives the set of transport-invariant flags from a consumer's spawn template.
symbols.description: Builds the union of unconditional head flags and body_key_override flags, then removes R6 swap-kind head defaults that are also capability-axis members, returning a sorted list.
